extraction.one_line_summary: DEMARE Arnaud won the sprint finish after LUTSENKO Alexey's solo attack from the breakaway was caught on the final circuit with under 1km to go.
tactical_patterns: Lotto-Soudal and Direct Energie controlled peloton tightly after Stage 1 lesson, keeping 4-man break under 1 minute at 31km.
LUTSENKO attacked solo from breakaway group on final circuit, showing Astana willing to sacrifice break cooperation for individual glory.
Sprinters' teams waited until 4.6km finishing circuit to reel in break, timing control tighter than typical flat stage.

rider_signals.DEMARE Arnaud: positive: Won sprint despite claiming heavy legs from hard opening 100km, suggesting superior recovery compared to pure sprinters
rider_signals.BOUHANNI Nacer: positive: Third place one month post-Yorkshire crash shows rapidly improving form trajectory toward Tour
rider_signals.BROWN Nathan: neutral: Cannondale allowed him to take virtual yellow in break, revealing team prioritizes stage hunting over GC protection
